attacking_style: Opportunistic attacker from breakaways or reduced groups, typically in final 15km. Prefers late-race moves after sustained hard tempo rather than explosive accelerations. Strong finishing speed to capitalize on premature attacks by others.
team_tactics: Versatile domestique who sacrifices personal ambitions for team leaders (Roglič, Benoot examples). When given freedom, attacks solo from mid-GC positions or animates from strong breakaways. Team rarely builds dedicated lead-out.
terrain_preference: Technical ITT courses (15-25km) and rolling classics terrain with late punchy climbs. Excels in record-pace breakaways and reduced bunch sprints after selective racing. Exposed on sustained steep climbs where pure climbers distance him in final kilometers.
vulnerability: Fades on final steep gradients after extended efforts (Mur de Huy base, Paris-Nice climbs). Exhaustible when performing extensive domestique duties early in stages. Struggles when required to follow explosive attacks rather than control tempo.
key_patterns: Two Tour de France stage wins via identical pattern: record-breaking breakaway pace then sprint finish.
Strong technical ITT ability: won Paris-Nice ITT, 2nd Tour de Suisse, negative split execution.
Superior finish speed in reduced groups, catching premature solo attacks in final meters (2 examples).
Willingly sacrifices personal GC/stage results for team objectives (3 documented instances).
Late-race solo attacks from GC positions around 15km to go, especially when in 7th-10th overall.
Performs best in Ardennes classics, repeatedly reaching final decisive groups on Mur de Huy.
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 Fleche Wallonne showed continued classics competitiveness but no progression from 2022 level. Pattern suggests plateaued at strong domestique/opportunist level rather than ascending to leader status.
